programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1972 Washington, 20-year-old Jody Loomis is discovered covered in blood by the roadside. She is brought to the hospital where she passes away from a bullet wound. It'll take over forty years for science to reveal her killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1989, 14-year-old Stephanie Isaacson's body is found in the Las Vegas desert. The case goes cold for decades until a nonprofit organization provides funding for the last DNA sample recovered to be used in genealogy research. Will it succeed?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2001, a 7-year-old girl is kidnapped and raped by an unknown assailant in Colorado. The victim lives in fear as the perpetrator is nowhere to be found - until nearly 20 years later, thanks to an exciting new forensic tool.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976 Wisconsin, 25-year-old David Schuldes and 24-year-old Ellen Matheys are murdered in a park. The case goes cold until 2015 when advancements in DNA science reignite the investigation.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1998, a wealthy woman disappeared from her home in Jackson, Mississippi. The only clue was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names but no specific instructions. The FBI struggled to find answers in the cryptic message.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, as drug profits grew, gangs ruled the streets of Chicago. Led by mastermind Jeff Fort, the dangerous, power-hungry El Rukn street gang attempted to conspire with Libya to perform acts of domestic terrorism for money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of being a doctor but she found herself an innocent victim of drug trafficking. Agents discovered a trail of drugs, hit men and murder as they attempted to solve her kidnapping.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished without a trace, along with the two men hired to guard it. Agents didn't know if the guards were willing participants or victims of an ambush. Suspicions grew that the crime was an inside job.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, two men used a sledgehammer to break into a Camden, New Jersey home. Police tracked the suspects to a nearby park but the criminals were able to escape after a nearly fatal shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Milwaukee teen was kidnapped by four gunmen who demanded $30,000 from his family for his safe return.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Chicago police and the FBI began to suspect that a law enforcement officer was responsible for the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Puerto Rican separatist group known as the FALN was suspected of setting off bombs in major U.S. cities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the spring of 1997, a killer preyed on senior citizens in the quiet town of River Parish, Louisiana.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In August 1992, a man returned to his Memphis home and discovered that his wife was missing. The husband, his friends, family and neighbors began to receive ransom calls.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A special task force was formed in the early 1990s to combat the flow of Colombia cocaine into New York. As part of an elaborate sting operation, one detective went undercover and infiltrated a drug gang connected to Pablo Escobar's Medellin cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy young woman was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband and several spurned lovers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On July 4, 1987, seven inmates broke out of a high-security prison in New Mexico. The daring escape was orchestrated by convicted murderer William Wayne Gilbert, who was serving a life sentence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a rash of home invasions plagued the Detroit area. Each time, masked men disguised as police officers broke in with assault rifles and stole money and jewelry. But when one homeowner shot back, the identity of the intruders was revealed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a parole officer was found shot dead in his car, the FBI, DEA and NYPD struggled to contain the mounting violence from drug gangs that continued to escalate. Authorities sought to put two ruthless drug kingpins behind bars for the crime.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert Hansen was a monster who preyed on the most vulnerable women in society - dehumanising them for his own twisted fantasies. Stranded with their killer in the vast Alaskan wilderness his victims had no chance of survival.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jorge Torrez was a predator capable of horrific acts against some of the most innocent in our society.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In April 1997, 27-year-old Rachel McGrath was stabbed to death by an unknown attacker in a car park. 24 hours later and 100 miles away, a terrified 17-year-old student flagged down a motorist, telling them she'd just escaped from a man.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: William Bonin is thought to have raped, tortured and murdered a minimum of twenty-one boys and young men.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Arthur Shawcross, known as the 'Genesee River Killer,' was a serial killer who murdered eleven women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Mansfield Jr. moved between Florida and Michigan, targeting vulnerable women and girls. Between 1975 and 1980, Mansfield Jr. murdered at least five victims, burying four of them at his family home in Spring Hill, Florida.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The Bayou Strangler deceived, bound, assaulted and murdered men, as he drifted across South Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Randy Kraft was a well-spoken, well-respected computer expert...and a prolific serial killer. Convicted in 1989 of murdering 16 men, he's suspected of having taken the lives of more than 60 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Joshua Wade had all the hall marks of a killer. We'll never understand the motives behind his random killings.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: James 'Porky' O'Rourke's addiction to violence means he is one of the most feared men in the UK prison system.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Rocky Beamon lures a woman into the woods and drowns her. Once in prison, he brutally kills two fellow inmates - and vows to kill again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace. Even though foul play is suspected, the leads don't pan out and the trail turns cold. Twenty years would pass before police are able to link a clump of hair discovered in an isolated area with the missing woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a discarded sleeping bag containing bloody sneakers and a purse, which eventually leads to the body of a young woman. A mark found on the victim's body enables police to track the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a residential community saw anything the day one of their neighbors was brutally murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three homicides on two continents looked like professional executions. Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic needed to find out if they were related and, if they were, who or what they had in common.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect - and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women from the same town were murdered in the same way, police feared a serial killer was on the loose. At first they thought the victims had nothing in common until they found tiny clues linking them to the same man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of a millionaire indicated robbery, but investigators wondered if there was something more.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who suffered from mental illness is found dead. Was it due to natural causes, suicide or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Emergency dispatch received a call from a man who said his girlfriend shot and killed herself. The autopsy revealed that the gunshot wound was not self-inflicted and the evidence on her body would give police a golden opportunity to catch her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A California teen went missing. Police suspected she'd run away until her body was discovered in a ditch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: California police had several suspects in the robbery and brutal murder of an elderly couple. Ten years later, a determined forensic scientist used material from a dentist's office to uncover ballistic evidence that had been overlooked.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of an Air Force officer was found dead in her bed with a plastic laundry bag near her face. Further investigation proved that the scene had been staged. Her death wasn't an accident, it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A neighbor watched as a man stabbed his wife, pushed her into the swimming pool and held her head under water.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two young children was found dead in her bedroom. Her death was ruled a suicide - but when investigators learned she had almost died in a house fire three years earlier, they decided to take another look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A computer crash pushed a manufacturing company to the brink of collapse. Investigators must determine if the crash was the result of a sabotage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police were puzzled by an obscure print found at a crime scene where one man had been killed and two teenage girls were seriously injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months passed, then years, and the crime was never solved.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the mail-order bride of a former army sergeant goes missing, investigators look into the deaths of the man's previous two wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after becoming pregnant by Dr. Maynard Muntzing, firefighter Michelle Baker's health quickly deteriorates. Eventually, she raises suspicions that Muntzing is doing her harm.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car is found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man who had committed crimes including murder was not caught. Almost 50 years later, advances in technology and handwriting analysis uncovered the criminal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young boy went missing, police organized the largest search in the history of their small town.

programme.sub-title: Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!
programme.desc: The Special Enforcement Detail prepares to take down a person with a felony warrant for theft and drug possession. Deputies investigating a disturbance in a field discover minors in possession of drugs and alcohol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A suspicious person is carrying large swords in his backpack. A traffic stop results in one passenger taking off on foot. A search warrant is executed for a grow operation. The arrest of a gang member who has drugs.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A sting operation results in the recovery of over 12 ounces of meth. An alarm goes off at a coffee shop. A report of a stolen vehicle at a gas station.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An intoxicated man runs through the streets in his underwear; a couple fights in their car; a couple won't stop fighting.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman is stopped for driving under the influence. A mentally unbalanced young man arms himself and refuses to cooperate with law enforcement.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police execute a search for heroin in a horrifically filthy house. A report of suspicious activity in a grocery store parking lot. Officers investigate an abandoned trailer.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police do a probation search of a known drug house. An officer gets information about a large marijuana grow operation. A deputy catches a drunk driver responsible for a hit-and-run accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A dangerous fugitive is wanted for assault and battery. A deputy stops an unregistered vehicle. A look at the daily procedures deputies must follow before going on patrol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An illegal marijuana grow operation is found in a mobile home. A deputy responds to a report of a loud and violent neighbor. A home burglary is interrupted by the homeowners.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies investigate a report of a woman throwing objects at passing cars. According to a domestic violence call, an intoxicated woman throws heavy objects at her husband.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies wishing to be part of the SWAT team face a testing procedure that is harsh and demanding.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Follow the Bike with No Light
programme.desc: A bicycle stopped for not having lights leads to the discovery of a stolen 27-foot trailer and its parolee occupants. Police respond to a brawl in a motel parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy is called to assist after a hit-and-run accident. A 9-1-1 caller reports a fight between a husband and wife. Deputies respond to a report from a mother that her vehicle was taken without permission by her daughter.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police search for a robbery suspect who has a felony warrant for his arrest. Deputies respond to a call from a mother who claims her son is attacking her. A traffic stop leads to a drug possession charge.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The investigation into a case of domestic abuse leads to the arrest of the abuser's friend on a $25,000 warrant. A deputy investigates two women who are parked behind a closed business late at night.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: While patrolling an area after a string of recent burglaries, a deputy finds a wanted man. A deputy is alerted to the location of two men with drugs firing off their guns.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: We follow Deputy Bryan Payne up close and personal on his night shift as he goes about a typical day for a deputy in El Dorado County.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police respond to a report of a stabbing at a housing complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The entire sheriff's department engages in an active school shooter training. A deputy sees through a string of lies after approaching a drug addict walking the streets at night. A transient verbally assaults pedestrians.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A man wanted for assault with a deadly weapon leads police on dramatic pursuit. A teen is stopped after stealing her father's car.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies pick up people with PRCS warrants of $150,000 or more.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy checks in on a woman who is on probation and is about to be evicted from the motel room she's living in. A man's neighbors claim he is driving drunk. A traffic stop leads to a drug charge.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of domestic abuse along with an assault with a deadly weapon. A traffic stop on a vehicle with an expired registration reveals three women who have some surprising drug habits.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A 9-1-1 call about a man threatening to kill his family. The investigation of a shoplifting case leads police to over 66 grams of black tar heroin. A domestic violence call.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond by boat to a report of an injured woman. A 92-year-old man with dementia becomes lost.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers try to clear out several hostile transients who are camping illegally. A deputy describes a mental game he uses to keep him sharp while on patrol. A man refuses to leave a car dealership.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County

programme.desc: A domestic violence call leads to a foot chase. Deputies respond to a burglary in progress. A deputy finds heroin on a man who was stumbling in the road.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A traffic stop reveals a man with stolen food and burglary tools who drives without a license. A deputy responds to a call about two intoxicated men. Two men are reportedly fighting at a department store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The leader of the Western El Dorado Narcotics Enforcement Team talks about the county's marijuana problem. The team takes down a hidden commercial marijuana grow house.

programme.desc: Deputies search a house for a domestic violence offender and his wife is pretending not to know his location; a probation search on a repeat offender.
